summary: Researchers at Argonne National Laboratory, including Taylor Harville, Rishu Khurana, Vitor F. Grizzi, and Cong Liu, have published a comprehensive survey and benchmarking study on the Variational Quantum Eigensolver algorithm, highlighting its significance for current Noisy Intermediate Scale Quantum hardware. Using a 72-qubit superconducting processor, they tested VQE adaptations designed to reduce circuit complexity and expand applicability to excited states in quantum chemistry. Chemistry-inspired ansatzes like Unitary Coupled Cluster (UCCSD) were evaluated for accuracy. Logical error rates reached 2.914% per cycle, supporting more reliable quantum computations. With approximately 6,000 physical qubits now available, these hybrid quantum-classical methods and robust quantum simulators offer a key path forward despite hardware limitations. (Reference: arXiv:2602.11384)
